---
title: "code_MOHAMMAD_FAIZ_FAM69"
author: "Faiz Mohammad"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

#load required packages
library(randomForest)
library(knitr)

#load data
train.data =read.csv("df_train.csv")
test.data =read.csv("df_test-1.csv")

#randomForest requires test data to have exact same categorical levels
#as training data we temporarily combine them to align factor levels

test.ids = test.data$ID
test.data$ID = NULL
test.data$price = 0

all.data = rbind(train.data, test.data)
cat.vars= c("neighbourhood_cleansed", "room_type", "host_is_superhost", 
              "instant_bookable", "host_identity_verified")

all.data[cat.vars] = lapply(all.data[cat.vars], as.factor)

train.data = all.data[1:nrow(train.data),]
test.data = all.data[(nrow(train.data)+1):nrow(all.data),]
test.data$price = NULL

#handle outliers (target variable is heavily right skewed due to extreme outliers: $5,556).
train.cleaned = train.data[train.data$price<=1000,]

#now model extrapolation bagging v. random forest
set.seed(123)

#Model 1: Bagging (m=p) 
bag.model = randomForest(price~., data = train.cleaned, mtry =16, importance = TRUE, ntree = 500)

#Model 2: Random forest (m =approx(p/3))
rf.model = randomForest(price~., data = train.cleaned, mtry =5, importance = TRUE, ntree = 500)

#Figure 1:Histogram of Prices (before cleaning)
hist(train.data$price,main = "Figure 1. Distribution of MSP Airbnb Nightly Prices", xlab = "Price (in USD)", col = "maroon")

#Table 1: Data Analysis Summary
eda.summary= summary(train.data[c("price", "accommodates", "bathrooms","dist_to_downtown")])
kable(eda.summary, caption = "Table 1: Exploratory Data Analysis Summary" )

#Table 2:Model Comparison (Bagging v. Random forest)
model.comparison= data.frame(Model = c("Bagging", "Random Forest"), Num_Variables_Tried_At_Split=c(16,5),
  Out_Of_Bag_MSE = c(tail(bag.model$mse, 1), tail(rf.model$mse, 1))
)
kable(model.comparison,caption = "Table 2: Model Comparison Bagging v. Random Forest")

#Figure 2: Variable Importance Plot(%IncMSE)
par(mar= c(5,15,6,2))
varImpPlot(rf.model, type = 1, main="", cex=.85)
title(main = "Figure 2: Variable Permutation Importance (%IncMSE)", 
      line = 1.5, cex.main = 1.1)

#Compare Out of Bag MSE
print(paste("Bagging OOB MSE:", tail(bag.model$mse,1)))
print(paste("Random Forest OOB MSE:", tail(rf.model$mse,1)))
#We see that Random Forest OOB MSE is less than Bagging OOB MSE

#Extract the permutation variable importance 
varImpPlot(rf.model, type = 1, main="Out of Bag Permutation Importance")

# Generate predictions for the test dataset
yhat.rf= predict(rf.model, newdata = test.data)

submission= data.frame(id = test.ids, price =yhat.rf)
file.name = "testing_predictions_MOHAMMAD_FAIZ_FAM69.csv"
write.csv(submission, file.name, row.names = FALSE)


```
